svg.text: LSF — LANGUE DES SIGNES FRANCAISE
HISTORY — ABBE DE L'EPEE AND DEAF EDUCATION
ABBE CHARLES DE L'EPEE (1712-1789)
1760: Abbe de l'Epee founds first free public school for the Deaf in Paris
Observed Deaf siblings signing; recognized it as language
Combined natural Old French Sign Language with artificial "methodical signs"
His approach: sign language + speech together (not pure oralism)
LSF SPREADS TO THE WORLD
1817: Laurent Clerc (Deaf French teacher) sails to America with Gallaudet
Clerc brings LSF to Hartford, CT; mixed with Martha's Vineyard SL = ASL
ASL: ~60% LSF-derived signs in core vocabulary
LSF model also exported to: Ireland, Russia, Scandinavia, Germany
THE ABBÉ SICARD AND AFTER
Roch-Ambroise Sicard succeeded l'Epee; trained Laurent Clerc
Paris school became INJS (Institut National de Jeunes Sourds) — still exists
1880 Milan: France voted to suppress LSF in its own schools
DGS HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
DGS AND FRENCH INFLUENCE
1778: Samuel Heinicke opens Leipzig school (oral method)
Despite oralism: Deaf communities in Germany signed independently
French SL model arrived via Prussian educational exchange in early 1800s
NAZI PERIOD AND AFTERMATH
1933-1945: ~17,000 Deaf Germans sterilized; DGS community devastated
Post-WWII: DGS reconstructed slowly; Hamburg becomes center
2002: DGS formally recognized • Hamburg University: major research center
LSF AND DGS TODAY
LSF: 2005 French law guarantees Deaf students right to LSF education
DGS: 2002 recognition; growing academic presence; DGS interpretation corps
Both: recovering communities after ~100 years of oralist suppression
LSF: founded modern Deaf education worldwide from Paris in 1760
The Abbe's gift: one teacher who listened, changed how the world hears
PLATE 64d ALT · LSF / DGS · HISTORY
